name: Ascent to Puigcerver by the PR-C 18 Les Borges del Camp - Puigcerver — circular from Riudecols
name_original: Subida a Puigcerver por el PR-C 18 Les Borges del Camp - Puigcerver — circular desde Riudecols
region_intro: Hiking around Riudecols offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, prominent viewpoints, and varied terrain. The region features a mix of Mediterranean vegetation, including pine forests and olive groves, alongside rocky outcrops and agricultural areas. Trails often lead to elevated positions, providing expansive views of the surrounding countryside. The area's topography supports a range of hiking experiences, from gentle strolls to more challenging ascents. Best hiking trails around Riudecols The most popular hiking route is Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop, a 5.0 miles (8.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This moderate route features distinctive rock formations and a scenic viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Rovelló and escaleras del Bruno — circular desde Alforja, a moderate 7.0 miles (11.3 km) path. This trail winds through forested areas and includes sections with notable elevation changes. Local hikers also love the Ascent to Puigcerver by the PR-C 18 Les Borges del Camp - Puigcerver — circular from Riudecols, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through hills and marked paths, often completed in about 4 hours 33 minutes. Hiking around Riudecols is defined by its hills, viewpoints, and varied Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Riudecols are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,100 reviews. More than 6,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Riudecols's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Riudecols?
A: The Riudecols area offers a wide selection of hiking routes, with over 300 trails to explore. This includes a diverse range of options, from easy strolls to more challenging ascents.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Riudecols?
A: Yes, Riudecols has nearly 90 easy hiking routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails often feature gentle terrain and shorter distances, allowing everyone to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

Q: What kind of views can I expect on hikes in Riudecols?
A: Hiking in Riudecols often leads to elevated positions, providing expansive views of the surrounding Mediterranean landscape. You'll see rolling hills, pine forests, olive groves, and rocky outcrops. Many routes offer prominent viewpoints, such as the one found on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1306534172/roques-del-barrufet-and-mirador-de-alforja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roques del Barrufet and Mirador de Alforja Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Riudecols?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Riudecols are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1307725615/el-rovello-and-escaleras-del-bruno-circular-desde-alforja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rovelló and escaleras del Bruno — circular desde Alforja&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path that winds through forested areas.

Q: What are some interesting natural attractions or landmarks to see while hiking in Riudecols?
A: The region around Riudecols is rich in natural and historical sites. You can discover unique rock formations, visit historical castles, or explore gorges and waterfalls. Notable attractions include the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/725611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Els Avencs de la Febró&lt;/a&gt; caves, the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1109069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg del Mas dels Frares Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5137425" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Miquel d'Escornalbou Castle and Monastery&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Riudecols?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, Riudecols offers over 30 difficult routes. These trails often feature significant elevation changes and longer distances, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1309126948/riudecols-puigvoltor-and-botarell-path-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riudecols, Puigvoltor, and Botarell Path Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 18 km.

Q: Is Riudecols a good destination for family-friendly hikes?
A: Yes, Riudecols is suitable for family hikes, especially with its numerous easy and moderate trails. Many routes are well-suited for children, offering opportunities to explore nature without overly strenuous climbs. Look for shorter, less steep options for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Riudecols?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on most hiking trails in the Riudecols area. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to check for any specific local regulations or protected areas that might have restrictions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Riudecols?
A: The hiking routes in Riudecols are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1,100 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscape, the well-maintained paths, and the stunning panoramic views from various viewpoints.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ruins accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, the area around Riudecols has several historical sites. You can hike to places like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/433933" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of La Mussara&lt;/a&gt; or explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/345742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Escornalbou Castle-Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, which are often integrated into longer hiking routes, offering a blend of nature and history.

Q: Is public transport available to reach trailheads in Riudecols?
A: While Riudecols is a rural area, some local bus services might connect to nearby towns. However, for direct access to many trailheads, driving is often the most convenient option. It's recommended to check local transport schedules for specific routes if you plan to rely on public transport.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Riudecols?
A: The Mediterranean climate of Riudecols makes hiking enjoyable for much of the year. Spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) are ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant natural colors. Summer can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winter offers cooler, crisp conditions, though some higher trails might be less accessible after heavy rains.
